sanskrit:
अर्जुन उवाच
किं तद् ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम।
अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते॥
transliteration:
arjuna uvāca
kiṃ tad brahma kim adhyātmaṃ kiṃ karma puruṣottama |
adhibhūtaṃ ca kiṃ proktam adhidaivaṃ kim ucyate ||
literal_meaning: Arjuna said: What is that Brahman? What is the self? What is action, O Supreme Person? What is declared to be the material manifestation? And what is called the demigods?
contextual_meaning: Arjuna, having moved from paralysis to curiosity, now seeks a cognitive map of ultimate reality. His questions reveal a meta-cognitive shift: he wants to know the categories of existence so he can locate himself and his duties within a coherent cosmos.
psychological_state: Post-decisional sense-making; the mind after resolving trauma seeks to re-conceptualize reality to prevent future disorientation.
trigger_scenario: After a panic attack or life-threatening diagnosis, the patient asks the therapist: 'What is really real? What part of me is truly me? What actions actually matter?'
anchor_text: Know the eternal self beyond body, mind and action.
